questions.question: The overjustification effect occurs when:

options.text: A person becomes more intrinsically motivated after receiving external rewards
External rewards decrease a person's intrinsic motivation for an activity
A person's arousal level exceeds the optimal level for performance
Higher-level needs are pursued before lower-level needs are met

questions.explanation: The overjustification effect occurs when providing external rewards for an intrinsically motivated behavior actually decreases the person's intrinsic motivation. The person begins to attribute their behavior to the external reward rather than their own interest.

questions.question: According to drive theory, behavior is motivated by the need to:

options.text: Achieve self-actualization
Maximize physiological arousal
Reduce internal tension and restore homeostasis
Satisfy cognitive curiosity

questions.explanation: Drive theory proposes that physiological needs create internal tensions (drives) that motivate behaviors aimed at reducing those tensions and restoring homeostasis -- the body's stable internal state. Hunger, thirst, and fatigue are classic examples of drives.

questions.question: Set point theory suggests that:

options.text: All people should weigh the same amount
The body has a natural weight range it actively works to maintain
Eating disorders are caused by an improperly set body weight target
Metabolism is unrelated to hunger regulation

questions.explanation: Set point theory proposes that each person's body has a natural weight range that it actively works to maintain through physiological mechanisms. When weight drops below this range, metabolism slows and hunger increases; the reverse occurs when weight exceeds the range.

questions.question: Leptin resistance in individuals with obesity means that:

options.text: Their bodies produce too little leptin
Their brains become less responsive to leptin's satiety signals
Ghrelin is no longer produced by the stomach
The hypothalamus stops regulating hunger entirely

questions.explanation: In leptin resistance, the brain becomes less responsive to leptin despite high levels being produced by fat cells. This means the normal satiety signal is weakened, contributing to chronic overeating even though the body has adequate fat stores.

questions.question: Which hormone plays a key role in sexual desire in both males and females?

options.text: Estrogen only
Oxytocin
Testosterone
Prolactin

questions.explanation: Testosterone is the primary androgen hormone that plays a key role in sexual desire in both males and females. While testosterone levels are much higher in males, it contributes to sexual motivation across genders.

questions.question: The James-Lange theory of emotion proposes that:

options.text: Physiological arousal and emotion occur simultaneously
We experience emotion first, which then causes physiological changes
We perceive physiological changes in our body, which then produce the emotional experience
Emotion requires both arousal and a cognitive label

questions.explanation: The James-Lange theory argues that we experience emotion as a result of perceiving physiological changes in our body. We feel sad because we cry, afraid because we tremble -- not the other way around.

questions.question: Paul Ekman's research on facial expressions found that:

options.text: All emotional expressions are culturally learned
Six basic emotions are universally recognized across cultures
Facial expressions have no connection to internal emotional states
Only positive emotions produce consistent facial expressions

questions.explanation: Ekman's cross-cultural research identified six basic emotions -- happiness, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, and disgust -- that are recognized and expressed through similar facial expressions across diverse cultures, suggesting a universal biological basis.

questions.question: In the Dutton and Aron bridge study, men on the swaying suspension bridge were more likely to call the female researcher because:

options.text: The bridge made them feel confident and assertive
They misattributed their fear-induced arousal to romantic attraction
The Cannon-Bard theory predicts attraction increases in dangerous settings
They experienced a cognitive appraisal of the researcher as threatening

questions.explanation: This classic study supported the Schachter-Singer two-factor theory. The men experienced physiological arousal from the frightening bridge and misattributed that arousal to attraction toward the woman, demonstrating how cognitive labeling of arousal shapes emotional experience.

questions.question: According to the Yerkes-Dodson law:

options.text: Higher arousal always leads to better performance
Performance is best at moderate levels of arousal
Intrinsic motivation is always superior to extrinsic motivation
Self-actualization can only occur when all other needs are met

questions.explanation: The Yerkes-Dodson law states that performance is optimal at moderate levels of arousal. Too little arousal leads to boredom and poor performance, while too much arousal leads to anxiety and reduced performance.

questions.question: Which theory of emotion emphasizes that our evaluation of a situation determines our emotional response, such that the same event can produce different emotions in different people?

options.text: James-Lange theory
Cannon-Bard theory
Cognitive appraisal theory
Schachter-Singer two-factor theory

questions.explanation: Cognitive appraisal theory, associated with Richard Lazarus, emphasizes that our evaluation (appraisal) of a situation relative to our goals and coping resources determines our emotional response. This explains why the same event can produce very different emotions in different people.
